danger_signals.visual_form: Dual health bars (two bars per character) decreasing from full
danger_signals.meaning: Player is taking damage and losing rounds; first to deplete both bars loses

danger_signals.visual_form: Character animation chains with rapid hit effects and visual feedback
danger_signals.meaning: Player is in danger of being combo-locked; opportunity to execute Combo Breaker
Imminent match loss; opponent executing finishing move on depleted opponent

danger_signals.visual_form: Extended hit sequence (20-80+ hits), sustained damage animation
danger_signals.meaning: High-damage finishing sequence; severe threat to remaining health bars

safety_signals.visual_form: Both health bars remaining visibly intact after blocking/defending
safety_signals.meaning: Player successfully defended against attack; position is temporarily safe

safety_signals.visual_form: Character animation breaks combo chain; leading health bar edge flashes white
safety_signals.meaning: Successfully escaped combo; advanced special moves now available with different commands

safety_signals.visual_form: Character positioned far from opponent on screen
safety_signals.meaning: Time to set up offense or defend; temporary reprieve from immediate threat

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Megacorporation organizational symbol (implied through created combatants)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Fulgore (cyborg enforcer), Riptor (cloned velociraptor), Glacius (abducted alien), Cinder (experimental subject)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tournament arena setting organized by Ultratech; dimensional rifts/technology implied

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Black Orchid (corporate saboteur), Chief Thunder (tribal/independent), Jago (spiritual warrior)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Characters fighting against or independent of Ultratech control

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Eyedol (boss creature), Spinal (undead warrior), Sabrewulf (lycanthropic curse)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Presence of Ultratech-created combatants (Fulgore, Riptor, Glacius, Cinder) in tournament roster
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: These characters fight as experimental test subjects; Fulgore specifically designed to stop Jago

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Eyedol's presence as escaped dimensional prisoner; interdimensional rifts mentioned in narrative
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss character battles; supernatural threat requiring tournament participation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fighting arena setting; dual health bar system indicating prolonged combat endurance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ritualized one-on-one combat; finishing moves (No Mercy, Humiliation, Ultra Combo) as spectacle elements

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinct character designs reflecting their origins (Tibetan monk Jago, Native American Chief Thunder, boxer T.J. Combo, etc.)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Character-specific finishing moves; varied move sets reflecting training/origin; Chief Thunder seeking missing brother

environmental_storytelling.example: Fulgore's cyborg appearance immediately communicates Ultratech origin; Glacius's alien form shows dimensional abduction; Cinder's flame body indicates experimental accident
Mix of Ultratech creations, independent fighters, and supernatural beings tells story of corporate overreach and dimensional instability
Character-specific No Mercy moves reflect individual backgrounds (martial arts, technology, supernatural, etc.)
Advanced character animation and visual diversity conveys technological sophistication and world-building through visual fidelity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Executes combos, initiates attacks, maintains stage control
Can execute Combo Breaker to escape sequence; leading health bar edge flashes white after successful breaker, enabling advanced special moves
Accessible to finishing moves (No Mercy/Danger Move); Ultra Combo available for match conclusion
Utilizes standard combos and special moves; demonstrates Ultratech experimental capabilities (for created combatants)
Represents dimensional danger; ultimate tournament obstacle

notes: Killer Instinct's visual communication system relies primarily on character design, health bar mechanics, and animation states to convey danger/safety rather than environmental hazards. The fighting paradigm itself is the world-state manifestation—the tournament arena and dual health bar system communicate that this is a high-stakes combat spectacle. Faction identity is conveyed through character origin and design: Ultratech's control manifests through its created/controlled combatants, while opposition emerges through independent characters (Black Orchid, Chief Thunder, Jago). The narrative of corporate overreach and dimensional instability is embedded in character rosters rather than stage environments. Pre-rendered CGI graphics were cutting-edge for 1994/1995, making visual distinction between character factions and origins a primary storytelling tool.
